scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, researched against developers.dwolla.com and the official Dwolla Go/Kotlin SDKs' request/response structs (github.com/kolanos/dwolla-v2-go, github.com/Dwolla/dwolla-v2-kotlin, fetched 2026-07-04). 4 legacy-parity read streams (customers, events, exchange_partners, business_classifications) unchanged, plus 15 new JSON-body write actions covering the full customer/funding-source/transfer/webhook-subscription/beneficial-owner lifecycle-mutation surface. Excluded: every endpoint that moves real money (create_transfer, mass payments — both destructive_admin, matching the pre-existing create_transfer exclusion decision), every endpoint requiring a multipart file upload (identity-verification documents, KBA is a question/answer flow with no create-time document but is itself excluded as session-scoped), the Dwolla Balance-tier Labels feature (a distinct, plan-gated sub-product), on-demand-authorization/IAV/funding-source-token minting (session tokens, not syncable records or persistent mutations), and single-object detail GETs that duplicate an already-covered list stream's record shape.

excluded.reason: single-customer detail; identical record shape to the customers list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

requires a per-customer partition (list every customer, then fan out one funding-sources request per id) that the top-level list streams do not need; out of scope for the read side this pass, matching legacy's own first-cut scoping. The create/update/remove funding-source WRITE actions are still covered below since a write's target customer_id/id comes from the caller's own record, not from this connector's own read side
same per-customer-partition reasoning as the funding-sources list; not modeled as a read stream this pass
single-scalar certification-status read; the same status the certify_beneficial_ownership write's response already carries, not a distinct syncable resource
per-customer-partition document metadata list; documents themselves are binary uploads (see POST below), out of scope this pass
identity-verification document upload requires multipart/form-data; the engine's write dialect has no multipart body_type
same transfer record shape as GET /transfers, scoped to one customer; not modeled as a separate stream
mass-payment status list scoped to a customer whose CREATE step (below) is excluded as destructive_admin; no way to ever populate a mass payment id to list from this connector
Dwolla Balance-tier Labels sub-product (fund reservation ledger), a distinct plan-gated feature legacy never touched
creating a label reserves/holds customer balance funds; Dwolla Balance-tier plan-gated feature, out of scope
mints a short-lived dwolla.js client token for browser-side funding-source tokenization; a session credential, not a syncable record
mints a short-lived Instant Account Verification session token for the dwolla.js IAV widget; a session credential, not a syncable record
single-funding-source detail; reachable only once an id is already known from a write response, not a distinct list-shaped resource to sync
single-scalar Dwolla Balance funding-source balance snapshot, not a syncable object list; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single-scalar micro-deposit status read; the same status the verify_micro_deposits write's response already carries
single-transfer detail; reachable only once an id is already known from elsewhere, not a distinct list-shaped resource this connector reads
initiates a real money transfer
single failed-transfer reason detail, not a distinct syncable resource; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
facilitator-fee sub-transactions of a single transfer, not a top-level syncable resource; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
initiates up to 5,000 real money transfers in a single batch; same reasoning as the single-transfer create
status polling for a mass payment whose only creation path is excluded as destructive_admin; no way to ever populate an id
same reasoning as the mass-payments GET: the create step is out of scope
same reasoning as the mass-payments GET
small (max 5-10), rarely-changing operational config list rather than business data; not modeled as a read stream this pass. The create/update/delete WRITE actions are still covered below
single-webhook-subscription detail; reachable only once an id is already known from a write response, not a distinct list-shaped resource to sync
individual webhook DELIVERY ATTEMPT log for a subscription, a debugging/audit trail rather than business data; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single delivery-attempt detail, same reasoning as the webhooks-for-subscription list
manually re-fires a previously-failed webhook delivery attempt; operational/debugging action, not a record mutation. Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single-beneficial-owner detail; reachable only once an id is already known from a write response, not a distinct list-shaped resource to sync
identity-verification document upload requires multipart/form-data; the engine's write dialect has no multipart body_type
per-owner document metadata list; documents themselves are binary uploads, out of scope this pass
single-event detail; identical record shape to the events list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-exchange-partner detail; identical record shape to the exchange_partners list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single open-banking exchange-session detail; a session/connection-flow resource, not a top-level business-data list. Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
creates an open-banking (Plaid/MX/Flinks/Finicity/Checkout.com) exchange session; a third-party-provider connection flow, not a plain data mutation. Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single-business-classification detail; identical record shape to the business_classifications list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
the API root resource, a HAL discovery document of top-level _links only; not a syncable record
mints a short-lived on-demand transfer authorization token/consent-text pair for immediate reuse in a funding-source creation call; a session credential, not a persistent syncable record
retrieves the same short-lived authorization token by id; still a session credential
retrieves a Knowledge-Based-Authentication question set for an in-progress identity-verification session; session-scoped, not a syncable record
submits KBA question/answer-id pairs to complete an identity-verification session; a one-shot session action tightly coupled to the customer-creation flow, not an independent record mutation
